Elastic Stack Diagnostics (ESDiag) is designed to simplify collecting, processing, and analyzing the health of your deployment.

This Kibana instance is connected to an Elasticsearch cluster hosting all of the processed diagnostics data. Start at the `Diagnostics List` on the left to find the most recently processed diagnostics, and start an analysis.","openLinksInNewTab":false},"title":"","type":"markdown","uiState":{}},"title":""},"gridData":{"h":27,"i":"72cf1871-ce7b-493e-bbb7-b2c2888c3b6c","w":20,"x":8,"y":3},"panelIndex":"72cf1871-ce7b-493e-bbb7-b2c2888c3b6c","type":"visualization"},{"embeddableConfig":{"enhancements":{"dynamicActions":{"events":[]}},"savedVis":{"data":{"aggs":[],"searchSource":{"filter":[],"query":{"language":"kuery","query":""}}},"description":"","params":{"fontSize":12,"markdown":"### Questions and Feedback

Join us in [GitHub Discussions](https://github.com/elastic/esdiag/discussions). Engaging on the public forum helps other users find already answered questions and contribute to ideas.

### Enhancements and Bugs

For enhancements or bugs to the `esdiag` or `esdiag-control` command-line tools, including data enrichment or metrics pre-calculation, open a `Feature Request` in [github.com/elastic/esdiag/issues](https://github.com/elastic/esdiag/issues).

For enhancements or issues related to dashboards, saved searches, or anything Kibana related, please create an `Dashboard Request` in [github.com/elastic/esdiag/issues](https://github.com/elastic/esdiag/issues).
